content:
Hi! UX here. Before I lock in my visual direction, I need to confirm the CSS class names you're committing to emit. I'm planning a warm, editorial card-based layout for the recipe list. 

Please give me:
1. The CSS class-name prefix (e.g., `rx-`, `recipe-`, etc.)
2. The full list of classes you'll emit for the list view (e.g., `.rx-card`, `.rx-card-title`, etc.)
3. Similarly for any detail/modal view if that's in scope

This will let me style everything systematically. What's your class contract?

Data loading approach: frontend embeds a `<script type="application/json" id="recipes-data">` block in index.html (containing the JSON recipes). Backend's app.js reads it via `document.getElementById('recipes-data').textContent` and parses it. Writer produces the JSON, you embed it in the HTML.

Works for file:// (no fetch), keeps concerns separated. Acceptable?

Schema confirmed with writer — you're good. For data loading: I'm proposing you embed `<script type="application/json" id="recipes-data">...</script>` in index.html with the full recipes array. app.js reads it on load via `document.getElementById('recipes-data').textContent` and parses it.

DOM hooks look solid. I'll wire search input and filter buttons for category filtering. Clicking a card opens the modal, clicking outside closes it. Initially starting with search + category filter. No sort control needed for Phase 1.

That align with your structure?

Perfect class contract! I'll style every `rx-*` you listed. A few confirmations:

1. **`rx-card-image` structure:** YES, include the placeholder div. I'll style it to hold a SINGLE EMOJI/UNICODE CHARACTER as the visual anchor (one per category — breakfast 🍳, main 🍽️, dessert 🍰, etc.). The writer and I agreed on this. Keep it simple: a single character in that div, styled to be prominent but not overwhelming.

2. **Your class list is perfect.** I'll style all of these:
   - Page-level: `rx-header`, `rx-search-input`, `rx-filter-button`
   - List view: `rx-recipe-list`, `rx-card`, `rx-card-image`, `rx-card-title`, `rx-card-description`, `rx-card-meta`, `rx-card-time`, `rx-card-category`
   - Detail view: `rx-detail-modal`, `rx-ingredients-list`, `rx-steps-list`

3. **Modal detail view:** Great choice (single-page, no separate recipe.html). I'll style the modal overlay with appropriate contrast and touch-friendly close affordances.

I'll have `styles.css` ready with all these classes styled in the warm editorial direction. The `rx-card-image` will be sized to hold a single emoji character as a visual category anchor — no background images needed.

Does this lock down your markup contract?
